snp4.15:4.0.935: “Fear has arisen from one who has taken up the rod:see the people engaged in strife.I will tell you of my sense of urgency,how I was stirred by a sense of urgency. (1) [183]
snp4.15:4.0.936: “Having seen the population tremblinglike fish in a pool with little water,having seen them hostile to one another,fear came upon me. (2)
snp4.15:4.0.937: “The world was insubstantial all around;all the directions were in turmoil.Desiring an abode for myself,I did not see [any place] unoccupied. (3)
snp4.15:4.0.938: “Having seen those hostile at the end,discontent came upon me.Then I saw the dart here,hard to see, nestled in the heart. (4)
snp4.15:4.0.939: “When one is struck by that dartone runs astray in all directions.But having drawn out that dart,one does not run, does not sink.”(5)
snp4.15:4.0.940: There the trainings are recited:“Whatever bondsthere are in the world,one should not be intent on them.Having entirely pierced through sensual pleasures,one should train for one’s own nibbāna. (6)
snp4.15:4.0.941: “One should be truthful, not impudent,without hypocrisy, devoid of divisive speech.Without anger, a muni should cross overthe evil of greed and miserliness. (7)
snp4.15:4.0.942: “One should vanquish sleepiness, torpor, and dullness;one should not keep company with heedlessness.A person whose mind is set on nibbānashould not persist in arrogance. (8) [184]
snp4.15:4.0.943: “One should not be led into false speech;one should not engender affection for form.One should fully understand conceit,and one should refrain from rashness. (9)
snp4.15:4.0.944: “One should not delight in the old;one should not form a liking for the new.One should not sorrow over what is diminishing;one should not be attached to an attraction. (10)
snp4.15:4.0.945: “I call greed the great flood,I call longing the rapids,the basis, compulsion,the swamp of sensuality hard to overcome. (11)
snp4.15:4.0.946: “Not deviating from truth, a muni,a brahmin, stands on high ground.Having relinquished all,he is indeed called peaceful. (12)
snp4.15:4.0.947: “One who truly is a knower, a Veda-master,having known the Dhamma, is independent.Living rightly in the world,he does not long for anything here. (13)
snp4.15:4.0.948: “One here who has crossed over sensual pleasures,the tie so hard to overcome in the world,who has cut off the stream, without bonds,does not sorrow, does not hope. (14)
snp4.15:4.0.949: “Dry up what pertains to the past;let there be nothing afterward.If you do not grasp in the middle,you will live in peace. (15)
snp4.15:4.0.950: “One who does not claim as ‘mine’anything at all here in name-and-form,who does not sorrow over what is nonexistent,truly does not lose out in the world. (16)
snp4.15:4.0.951: “One for whom nothing is takenas ‘this is mine’ or ‘[this belongs] to others,’not finding anything to be taken as ‘mine,’does not sorrow, thinking: ‘It is not mine.’ (17) [185]
snp4.15:4.0.952: “Not bitter, not greedy,without impulse, everywhere the same —when asked about one who is unshakable,I call that the benefit. (18)
snp4.15:4.0.953: “For one without impulse, who understands,there is no activation at all.Abstaining from instigation,he sees security everywhere. (19)
snp4.15:4.0.954: “The muni does not speak of himselfas among equals, inferiors, or superiors.Peaceful, without miserliness,he does not take, does not reject” —so said the Blessed One. (20)
